events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess scenario: NM within 1 1/4 in of stud face without nail plate. NEC 300.4 protection from physical damage. For NM cable (334) run through bored holes in studs or notches, if within 1 1/4 in of nearest edge of framing member, must be protected by steel plate or equivalent to protect from nails/screws. So non-compliant. Decision fail. Risk high? Could be medium/high. Residential rough-complete. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 80? value 0. Findings: NM cable installed less than 1.25 in from stud face without nail plate, vulnerable to penetration. Actions: install 1/16 in steel nail plates or relocate cable >1.25 in from stud face. Rationale: NEC 300.4(A)(1)/(D) requires protection for cables within 1.25 in of framing edge. References NEC 300.4, 334.17 maybe. Need include all fields. sound_source etc default empty string/0. order empty list, workable empty list. Ensure valid JSON.

events.text:

  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": ["NM cable is installed within 1¼ in. of the stud face without a protective nail plate", "Cable is exposed to potential nail/screw penetration before finish materials are installed"],
  "actions": ["Install a 1/16-in. minimum steel nail plate or equivalent protection over the cable where it is within 1¼ in. of the stud face", "Alternatively, reroute the cable to maintain at least 1¼ in. clearance from the nearest edge of the framing member"],
  "rationale": "NEC 300.4 requires cables installed through bored holes or notches in framing and located within 1¼ in. of the nearest edge to be protected by a steel plate or equivalent to guard against nail/screw penetration. The observed NM cable lacks that protection, so it does not comply.",
  "references": ["NEC 300.4(A)(1) and 300.4(D)", "NEC 334.17"]
}
